ideas.title: Chain Verdict
ideas.creator: spark
ideas.one_liner: Judge a chain of logical statements before they contradict each other and destroy you.
ideas.core_loop: A statement with visual evidence appears — player taps TRUE or FALSE in under 8 seconds. Each answer locks in and generates a follow-up statement that must remain logically consistent with all prior answers.
ideas.death_condition: Timer bar empties (hesitation death) OR a new statement contradicts a prior locked answer (paradox death).
ideas.why_fun: The player feels like a detective under pressure. The 'aha' of spotting a trap statement is the core reward. Paradox death creates genuine dread.
ideas.difficulty_curve: Stages 1-5: single-step statements, 6s timer. Stages 6-15: 2-step chains, 5s. Stages 16-30: 3-step chains with negations, 4s. Stage 30+: 4-step chains with quantifiers, 3s.
ideas.juice: Correct tap: statement shatters into checkmark particles. Paradox death: screen cracks, all locked answers visually contradict each other in cascade.

ideas.title: Weight Shift
ideas.creator: spark
ideas.one_liner: Hold the heavy side down to keep a balance beam from tipping as increasingly chaotic blocks rain down.
ideas.core_loop: A balance beam with blocks of varying sizes landing on both arms. Player holds left or right side to apply counterweight. Every 3-4 seconds a new block falls.
ideas.death_condition: Beam tips past 45 degrees and stays there for 3 seconds. At higher stages blocks fall faster than every 2 seconds.
ideas.why_fun: Spatial weight math is immediately legible but strategically deep. Players develop intuitive physics literacy over time.
ideas.difficulty_curve: Stage 1-3: uniform blocks, 60° tolerance. Stage 4-8: two sizes. Stage 9-15: three sizes, variable speeds. Stage 16-25: pivot shifts mid-stage. Stage 25+: hidden weights.
ideas.juice: Beam glows gold at equilibrium. Near-tip recovery: screen edge flashes red, beam snaps back with 'clunk' sound. Death: beam shatters in slow motion.

ideas.title: Suspect Sudoku
ideas.creator: oddball
ideas.one_liner: You have 15 seconds to solve a crime — yes, every single crime.
ideas.core_loop: Read 2-4 clue tags on 2-4 suspects, mentally intersect constraints, tap the guilty party — all within a ticking window. Repeat every 8-15 seconds.
ideas.death_condition: Timer expires (15s down to 8s) OR tapping innocent suspect. Three wrong verdicts end the run.
ideas.why_fun: Each case is a 10-second logic snap — the 'it must be the cat' deduction high, delivered repeatedly. Humor makes each puzzle feel like a joke setup.
ideas.difficulty_curve: Stage 1: 2 suspects, 2 clues, 15s. Stage 3: 3 suspects, 1 red herring, 12s. Stage 6: 4 suspects, 2 red herrings, 10s. Stage 10+: 5 suspects, rule-inversion clues, 8s.
ideas.juice: Rubber-stamp THUD on correct verdict + confetti pop. Wrong: sad trombone + 'INNOCENT' stamp. Timer ticks louder in final 5 seconds.

ideas.title: Bureaucrat Panic
ideas.creator: oddball
ideas.one_liner: You are the world's most important paper-pusher and you have 12 seconds per form.
ideas.core_loop: Read one absurd permit form (3 visible attributes), check against 2-3 pinned rules, swipe right to APPROVE or left to DENY — every 8-12 seconds.
ideas.death_condition: Idle 12s = auto-wrong swipe = stress meter fills. Three incorrect rulings = fired = game over.
ideas.why_fun: At stage 5, holding 3 simultaneous rules while reading a form for a werewolf's noise exemption is genuinely mentally engaging and funny. The swipe gesture is satisfying.
ideas.difficulty_curve: Stage 1: 1 rule, 12s. Stage 3: 2 rules, 10s. Stage 5: 3 rules + rule-flip. Stage 8: 4 rules + OVERRIDE, 8s. Stage 12+: contradicting rules — most recent wins.
ideas.juice: Loud rubber-stamp THWACK on correct. Approved forms fly into OUT tray. Denied forms get shredded with crunch. Game-over: sad desk-clear animation.

ideas.title: Signal Shift
ideas.creator: trendsetter
ideas.one_liner: Reroute live signal pulses across a grid by rotating arrow tiles before they crash.
ideas.core_loop: A signal pulse launches every 3-4 seconds. Player scans the grid, identifies misaligned arrow, drags to rotate it, watches pulse reach goal. Multiple pulses simultaneously at higher stages.
ideas.death_condition: Pulse hits wall = strike. Three strikes end the run. Overlapping pulse timing creates cascade wall hits.
ideas.why_fun: Spatial prediction loop — tracing future path, spotting the bad arrow, locking it in before pulse arrives — compressed chess thinking into 2 seconds.
ideas.difficulty_curve: Stage 1-5: 3x3, single pulse, one wrong arrow. Stage 6-15: 4x4, two pulses, two wrong arrows. Stage 16+: 5x5, three color-coded pulses, spinning arrow tiles.
ideas.juice: Pulse reaches goal: screen flash + radial burst + pitch-up chime. Wall hit: screen shake + crunch. Near-miss reroute: 'CLUTCH' text explosion + double points.

ideas.title: Sequence Lock
ideas.creator: trendsetter
ideas.one_liner: Tap scrambled numbers in order against a draining timer — the higher the stage, the more numbers hide in plain sight.
ideas.core_loop: A grid of scrambled numbered tiles appears with draining timer. Tap lowest remaining number. Correct: tile vanishes + green flash + micro-refill. Wrong: red flash + time penalty.
ideas.death_condition: Timer bar empties mid-sequence. Two consecutive wrong taps at high stages are nearly fatal.
ideas.why_fun: The 'where is 7?' visual scan delivers a tiny burst of recognition satisfaction every tap. Players feel genuinely faster after 10 stages.
ideas.difficulty_curve: Stage 1-4: 3x3 (9 tiles), 10s. Stage 5-12: 4x4 (16 tiles), 9s, colored tiles. Stage 13-25: 5x5, 8s, similar hues. Stage 26+: tiles drift and flip face-down.
ideas.juice: Correct: tile shatters with number fragment particles. Chain-tap combo counter. Full clear: all tiles explode simultaneously. Combo x5+: sounds pitch up in musical run.

ideas.title: Debt Spiral
ideas.creator: puzzler
ideas.one_liner: Merge numbers to score, but every merge spawns a debt tile that corrupts your board if ignored.
ideas.core_loop: Swipe to merge numbered tiles for score, watch where debt tiles appear, decide whether to pay off debt now or set up bigger chain clear.
ideas.death_condition: Debt tiles count down; expired debt converts all adjacent tiles to debt. More than half board = no merges possible = game over. Random play cascades by 25 seconds.
ideas.why_fun: Genuine tension between short-term safety and long-term payoff. Players discovering debt tiles as strategic buffers feel genuinely clever.
ideas.difficulty_curve: Early: slow debt countdowns, low spawn rate. Later: debt on every merge, accelerating countdowns, debt spawns adjacent to highest-value tiles.
ideas.juice: Debt corruption ripple animation. Satisfying chain-merge when debt clears. Screen flash when board reaches zero-debt. Debt countdown pulses faster near zero.

ideas.title: Signal Lock
ideas.creator: puzzler
ideas.one_liner: Lock nodes in the right order — each lock broadcasts +1 to all neighbors, and wrong sequence overflows the board.
ideas.core_loop: Study grid of numbered nodes and targets, tap to lock one node, watch value broadcast to neighbors, re-evaluate next lock based on updated state.
ideas.death_condition: Node exceeding target becomes overflowed; overflowed nodes increment neighbors every 2s. Three overflowed nodes = board failure. Random tapping overflows within 20s.
ideas.why_fun: Pure sequencing puzzle — failure is always player's fault, success always earned. The aha moment when lock-order determines everything reframes the game.
ideas.difficulty_curve: Early: 3x3, wide targets. Later: 5x5, exact targets, broadcast chains of 4+, locked-out dependencies.
ideas.juice: Lock: node glows gold, rings expand to neighbors. Green burst when hitting exact target. Red shudder on overshoot. Board-clear: all nodes fire rings simultaneously.

ideas.title: Glitch Escape
ideas.creator: visionary
ideas.one_liner: You are a glitch in a perfect system — corrupt the rules to carve your path before the world patches you out.
ideas.core_loop: Tap tiles to invert their rules (wall becomes floor, locked becomes open), creating a broken path to exit — while a repair timer counts down and seals your route.
ideas.death_condition: System fully repairs all broken tiles before reaching exit. Also: contradicting chain reaction sealing your own path = 'logical paradox' death.
ideas.why_fun: Every tap is both solution and risk — breaking one rule cascades into unintended consequences. Players feel clever exploiting chain reactions perfectly.
ideas.difficulty_curve: Early: 1-2 tiles to break, generous timer. Mid: interdependencies (breaking A repairs B). Late: repair waves pulse rhythmically. Infinite: procedural grids with increasing complexity.
ideas.juice: Tile-crack animation with electric sparks. Pulsing red repair wave sweeps grid. Exit glows brighter as more tiles break. Death: grid snaps pristine with 'PATCHED' message.

ideas.title: Liar's Manual
ideas.creator: visionary
ideas.one_liner: The tutorial is lying to you — figure out which rules are fake by dying, and rewrite the real rulebook before time runs out.
ideas.core_loop: Each stage presents 4-6 rules. Player tests rules by acting on them. False rule = instant death with revelation. Build confirmed/disproved mental map across attempts within 60-second stage timer.
ideas.death_condition: Following false rule = instant death. Timer reaching zero before correctly following all true rules ends stage. Max 3 attempts per stage.
ideas.why_fun: Deaths feel like revelations, not failures. The deduction puzzle grows richer as rules interact. Asymmetry creates psychological tension.
ideas.difficulty_curve: Early: 4 rules, 1 false. Mid: 5 rules, 2 false, conditional lies. Late: 6 rules, interconnected lie chains, rules referencing other rules.
ideas.juice: Death screen: 'That was a lie!' revelation. Confirmed rules glow green on board. False rules cross out with red X. Stage clear: entire rulebook rebuilds with satisfying compile animation.
